examTitle: Chapter 9: Centre of Mass and Collision - Practice Set
source: A Quick Review of E-PCM for Engineering Entrance - Physics
subject: Physics

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A block of mass m moving at a velocity v collides with another block of mass 2m at rest. The lighter block comes to rest after collision. Find the coefficient of restitution:
questions.explanation: From momentum: mv = 2m v2 => v2=v/2. Using e=(v2-v1)/(u1-u2)=(v/2-0)/(v-0)=1/2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two blocks of mass 10 kg and 30 kg are placed along a vertical line. The first block is raised through a height of 7 cm. By what distance should the second mass be moved to raise the centre of mass by 1 cm?
questions.explanation: m1*dy1+m2*dy2=(m1+m2)dy_cm => 10*7+30*dy2=40*1 => dy2=-1 cm (downward).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two persons A and B of masses 50 kg and 60 kg sit at ends of a 4 m platform of mass 40 kg on smooth ice. If both come to the middle, approximately how far does the boat move on ice?
questions.explanation: Using COM fixed: 50(2+x)+60(-2+x)+40x=0 => x=20/1.5=13.33 cm approx.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A man of mass M stands at one end of a plank of length L on a frictionless surface. He walks to the other end. If mass of plank is 3M, distance moved by man relative to ground is:
questions.explanation: Shift of man wrt ground = L - plank shift. Plank shift = (M/(M+3M))L=L/4 opposite. So man moves 3L/4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two balls of equal mass have a head-on collision with speed 6 m/s. If coefficient of restitution is 1/3, speed of each ball after impact will be:
questions.explanation: For equal masses in head-on with opposite equal speeds u, each final speed magnitude = e*u = (1/3)*6 = 2 m/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A shell is fired with velocity v at angle theta. At highest point it explodes into two equal masses. One piece retraces path to cannon. Speed of other piece immediately after explosion is:
questions.explanation: At top, shell horizontal speed=v cos theta. If one piece reverses with speed v cos theta, momentum conservation for equal masses gives other piece speed 3v cos theta forward.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two particles P and Q initially at rest move towards each other under mutual attraction. At an instant speed of P is v and speed of Q is 2v. Speed of centre of mass is:
questions.explanation: Only internal forces act, total momentum stays zero from initial rest. Hence v_cm=0 always.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body of mass m moving with speed v collides elastically head-on with body 2m at rest. Ratio of kinetic energy of colliding body before and after collision is:
questions.explanation: For m colliding elastically with 2m at rest: v1=(m-2m)/(m+2m)u=-u/3. KE ratio before/after for body1 = u^2:(u^2/9)=9:1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A sphere of mass m with speed u hits another stationary sphere of same mass. If e is coefficient of restitution, ratio of velocities of two spheres after collision is:
questions.explanation: For equal masses with u2=0: v1=(1-e)u/2, v2=(1+e)u/2. Ratio v1/v2=(1-e)/(1+e).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Three identical spheres each of mass 1 kg are placed touching each other on a straight line at points P,Q,R. Distance of centre of mass from P is:
questions.explanation: Take P at 0, Q at PQ, R at PR. x_cm=(0+PQ+PR)/3=(PQ+PR)/3.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: COM of masses 1 kg,2 kg,3 kg is at (1,2,3) and COM of masses 3 kg,2 kg is at (-1,3,-2). Where should a 5 kg particle be placed so that COM of entire system is that of first system?
questions.explanation: Using weighted COM condition about required point gives position (3,1,8).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A uniform disc of radius R has a smaller disc of diameter R cut from edge. COM of remaining part is at distance from original centre:
questions.explanation: By subtraction of masses (hole as negative mass), COM shift for this geometry comes out R/8 from original centre.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If net external force on a system is zero, which may vary?
questions.explanation: With zero external force, momentum and COM velocity remain constant, but position can change uniformly with time.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: For a rocket moving in free space, fraction of mass to be disposed to attain speed equal to two times exhaust speed is:
questions.explanation: Rocket equation: v=ve ln(m0/m). Given v=2ve => m/m0=e^-2=0.135. Disposed fraction=1-0.135=0.865 ~ 0.86.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A 2 kg block on table is hit by 5 g bullet at 150 m/s and bullet sticks. Block slides 2.7 m to stop. Friction force is:
questions.explanation: Post-collision speed V=mu/(M+m)=0.005*150/2.005~0.374 m/s. Work by friction=f*s=1/2(M+m)V^2 => f~0.052 N.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A man of mass M stands at one end of plank length L on frictionless surface. If plank mass is 3M, distance man moves relative to ground is:
questions.explanation: Same as Q4; answer 3L/4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If a body of mass m collides head-on elastically with another identical body at rest, velocity of second body after collision will be:
questions.explanation: In elastic collision of identical masses, velocities exchange. So second body gets u.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A 2 kg metal ball at 36 km/h has head-on collision with a stationary 3 kg ball and they move together. Loss in KE is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A 1 kg body at rest explodes into masses in ratio 1:1:3. Two equal masses move perpendicular with 30 m/s. Speed of heavier part is:
questions.explanation: Masses: 0.2,0.2,0.6 kg. Momentum of two small parts are perpendicular: each 0.2*30=6, resultant=6sqrt2. Heavy part momentum same opposite => v=6sqrt2/0.6=10sqrt2 m/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If kinetic energy of a body is increased by 300%, percentage change in momentum is:
questions.explanation: KE increases by 300% means KE_new=4 KE_old. Momentum p proportional sqrt(KE), so p_new=2p_old => 100% increase.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A stationary particle explodes into two particles of masses m1 and m2 moving opposite with v1 and v2. Ratio of their kinetic energies E1/E2 is:
questions.explanation: From momentum: m1v1=m2v2. E1/E2=(m1v1^2)/(m2v2^2)=m1/m2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The centre of mass of a system of particles does not depend on:
questions.explanation: COM is geometric-mass distribution property; it depends on masses and positions, not directly on forces.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body of mass a moving with velocity b strikes body of mass c and gets embedded into it. Velocity of combined system after collision is:
questions.explanation: Based on provided key for this set, correct marked option is B.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body of mass M at rest explodes into three masses; two pieces of mass M/4 each move in perpendicular directions with 3 m/s and 4 m/s. Speed of third piece is:
questions.explanation: Resultant momentum of first two: (M/4)*sqrt(3^2+4^2)=(M/4)*5=5M/4. Third mass is M/2, so speed=(5M/4)/(M/2)=2.5 m/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: An object of mass 40 kg with velocity 4 m/s collides perfectly inelastically with object of mass 60 kg moving at 2 m/s in same direction. Loss in energy is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A gun fires 50 g bullet with velocity 30 m/s. Due to this, gun recoils at 1 m/s. Mass of gun is:
questions.explanation: Momentum conservation: m_b v_b = M_g v_g => 0.05*30 = M*1 => M=1.5 kg.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A neutron makes head-on elastic collision with stationary deuteron. Fractional energy loss of neutron is:
questions.explanation: For m colliding elastically with M at rest, fraction retained = ((m-M)/(m+M))^2. Here m=1,M=2 => retained=1/9, loss=8/9.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two stones of masses 4 kg and 8 kg move towards each other with speeds 4 m/s and 10 m/s. Velocity of centre of mass is:
questions.explanation: Take opposite directions: v_cm=(4*4-8*10)/(12)=(-64)/12=-5.33 m/s; speed 5.3 m/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The basic principle of rocket propulsion is based on:
questions.explanation: Rocket thrust is reaction to expelled gases, i.e., Newton third law / momentum conservation.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: An object of mass 3M at rest splits into three equal fragments. Two fragments have velocities v i-hat and -v j-hat. Velocity of third fragment is:
questions.explanation: Total momentum zero. Third momentum must be negative of sum of first two: p3=-(Mv i + (-Mv j))=M(-vi+vj). As per provided key option A is marked in this set.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A hollow sphere is held suspended. Sand is poured into it in stages. Centre of gravity of sphere:
questions.optionA: first falls and then rises to original position
questions.optionB: first rises and then falls to original position
questions.explanation: Initially C.G. shifts downward as sand fills lower part; with full filling it returns toward geometric centre, so first falls then rises.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A projectile moving at 20 m/s at highest point breaks into equal parts by internal explosion. One part moves vertically up at 30 m/s wrt ground. Other part moves at:
questions.explanation: Before explosion momentum = m*20 horizontal. One half has velocity 30 vertical. Using vector momentum conservation for equal halves gives other half speed 50 m/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If force on a rocket moving with velocity 300 m/s is 210 N, rate of combustion of fuel is:
questions.explanation: Thrust F = mdot * v_e (effective exhaust speed given 300 m/s). mdot = 210/300 = 0.7 kg/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A ball of mass 250 g moving at 20 m/s is stopped by a force acting for 0.1 s. Impulsive force is:
questions.explanation: Average force=Delta p/Delta t=(0.25*20)/0.1=50 N, not in A/B/C. So D.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two identical balls A and B collide head-on elastically. If initial velocities are +0.5 m/s and -0.3 m/s, then velocities after collision are:
questions.explanation: For elastic collision of identical masses, velocities exchange. So A gets -0.3 and B gets +0.5 m/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Object A (2 kg) with velocity 3 m/s collides head-on with object B (1 kg) moving opposite at 4 m/s. They coalesce. Common velocity is:
questions.explanation: Take A positive: v=(2*3+1*(-4))/(3)=2/3 m/s.
